# Deploy OpenClaw on AWS EC2

This runbook guides you through deploying [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw) on a standalone AWS EC2 instance using Terragrunt and OpenTofu. OpenClaw is an open-source AI agent framework that runs 24/7 and can autonomously execute tasks like checking you into flights, responding to messages, and booking appointments.

## What you'll deploy

You'll deploy a self-contained Terragrunt configuration that creates:

1. A dedicated **VPC** with a public subnet and internet gateway
2. An **EC2 instance** (Graviton ARM by default) running OpenClaw in Docker
3. **Tailscale** mesh VPN for secure private access to the OpenClaw UI
4. An **Elastic IP** for stable SSH access to the instance
5. A **security group** restricting access to SSH only (OpenClaw is accessed via Tailscale)

Estimated monthly cost: **~$25/month** (t4g.medium + 30GB EBS + Elastic IP).

## Pre-flight checks

Before you begin, ensure you have the required tools installed and are properly authenticated.

### Authenticate to GitHub

Authenticate to GitHub so the runbook can clone repos, create pull requests, and create releases.

<GitHubAuth
  id="gh-auth"
  title="Authenticate to GitHub"
  description="Authenticate to GitHub to access your infra-catalog and infra-live repositories."
/>

### Authenticate to AWS

First, authenticate to AWS. This runbook uses AWS SSO (IAM Identity Center) for authentication.

<AwsAuth
  id="aws-auth"
  title="Authenticate to AWS"
  description="Authenticate to the AWS account where you want to deploy OpenClaw."
  ssoStartUrl="https://d-9267d384ee.awsapps.com/start"
  ssoRegion="us-west-2"
  defaultRegion="us-west-2"
/>

### Install mise

Verify that mise is installed. If not, install it from [mise.jdx.dev](https://mise.jdx.dev/getting-started.html).

<Check
  id="check-mise"
  command="mise --version && mise self-update --yes;"
  title="Check mise Installation"
  description="We recommend `mise` as a tool version manager that can install and manage Terragrunt, OpenTofu, and other tools. This checks that mise is installed and up to date."
  successMessage="mise is installed and up to date!"
  failMessage="mise is not installed. Install it from https://mise.jdx.dev/getting-started.html"
/>

### Install Required Tools

Once mise is installed, run the following command to install all required tools:

<Command
  id="install-tools"
  command="mise use terragrunt@latest opentofu@latest awscli@latest granted@latest"
  title="Install Required Tools via mise"
  description="Installs Terragrunt, OpenTofu, AWS CLI, and Granted using mise"
  successMessage="All tools installed successfully!"
  failMessage="Failed to install tools. Check the logs for details."
/>

## Generate a Tailscale Auth Key

Generate a Tailscale auth key so the EC2 instance can join your Tailnet automatically.

Go to [Tailscale Admin Console](https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys) > Settings > Keys > Generate auth key. Enable "Reusable" if you plan to redeploy.

<Inputs id="tailscale-config">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: TailscaleAuthKey
    type: string
    description: "Tailscale pre-authentication key. Generate at: Tailscale Admin > Settings > Keys > Generate auth key"
    validations:
      - required
```
</Inputs>

## infra-catalog: Generate the OpenTofu Module

### Clone infra-catalog

Clone the **infra-catalog** repo where the OpenTofu module will be written.

<GitClone
  id="clone-catalog"
  gitHubAuthId="gh-auth"
  title="Clone infra-catalog"
  description="Clone the repository where the OpenTofu module will be written."
  prefilledUrl="https://github.com/josh-padnick/infra-catalog"
/>

### Configure and generate the OpenTofu module

<Template
  id="gen-module"
  path="templates/infra-catalog-module"
  target="worktree"
/>

### Create a pull request on infra-catalog

Push the generated module and open a pull request.

<GitHubPullRequest
  id="pr-catalog"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  inputsId="gen-module"
  title="Create Pull Request on infra-catalog"
  description="Push the generated OpenTofu module and open a PR against infra-catalog."
  prefilledPullRequestTitle="Add {{ .inputs.ModuleName }} module"
  prefilledPullRequestDescription="Add the {{ .inputs.ModuleName }} OpenTofu module.\n\nGenerated by the OpenClaw runbook."
  prefilledBranchName="add-{{ .inputs.ModuleName }}-module"
/>

### Merge the pull request

Review and merge the pull request on GitHub, then verify it's been merged.

<Check
  id="check-pr-merged"
  path="checks/check-pr-merged.sh"
  title="Verify PR is Merged"
  description="Check that the infra-catalog pull request has been merged to main."
  successMessage="PR is merged!"
  failMessage="PR is not yet merged. Merge it on GitHub and try again."
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

### Create a release on infra-catalog

Tag the infra-catalog repo so Terragrunt can pull a pinned version of the module.

<Command
  id="release-catalog"
  path="scripts/release-catalog.sh"
  title="Create Release"
  description="Create a release on the infra-catalog repo."
  successMessage="Release created!"
  failMessage="Failed to create release. Check the logs for details."
  inputsId="gen-module"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

## infra-live: Generate the Terragrunt Unit

### Clone infra-live

Clone the **infra-live** repo where the Terragrunt unit (terragrunt.hcl) will be written.

<GitClone
  id="clone-live"
  gitHubAuthId="gh-auth"
  title="Clone infra-live"
  description="Clone the repository where the Terragrunt unit (terragrunt.hcl) will be written."
  prefilledUrl="https://github.com/josh-padnick/infra-live"
/>

### Generate the Terragrunt unit into infra-live

<Template
  id="gen-unit"
  path="templates/infra-live-unit"
  target="worktree"
  inputsId={["gen-module", "tailscale-config"]}
/>

### Create a pull request on infra-live

Push the generated Terragrunt unit and open a pull request.

<GitHubPullRequest
  id="pr-live"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  inputsId="gen-unit"
  title="Create Pull Request on infra-live"
  description="Push the generated Terragrunt unit and open a PR against infra-live."
  prefilledPullRequestTitle="Add {{ .inputs.InstanceName }} Terragrunt unit"
  prefilledPullRequestDescription="Add Terragrunt configuration for {{ .inputs.InstanceName }}.\n\nGenerated by the OpenClaw runbook."
  prefilledBranchName="add-{{ .inputs.InstanceName }}-unit"
/>

### Merge the infra-live pull request

Review and merge the pull request on GitHub, then verify it's been merged.

<Check
  id="check-live-pr-merged"
  path="checks/check-live-pr-merged.sh"
  title="Verify infra-live PR is Merged"
  description="Check that the infra-live pull request has been merged to main."
  successMessage="PR is merged!"
  failMessage="PR is not yet merged. Merge it on GitHub and try again."
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

## Deploy OpenClaw

### Set the Tailscale auth key

Before running Terragrunt, set the Tailscale auth key as an environment variable in your terminal. The leading space prevents the command from being saved in shell history.

<TemplateInline id="tailscale-env-snippet" inputsId="tailscale-config">
```bash
 export TAILSCALE_AUTH_KEY="{{ .inputs.TailscaleAuthKey }}"
```
</TemplateInline>

Now preview the changes with a plan, then apply.

<Command
  id="terragrunt-plan"
  path="scripts/terragrunt-plan.sh"
  title="Terragrunt Plan"
  description="Preview the infrastructure changes that will be made to deploy OpenClaw."
  successMessage="Plan completed successfully! Review the changes above."
  failMessage="Terragrunt plan failed. See logs for details."
  inputsId={["gen-unit", "gen-module"]}
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

<Command
  id="terragrunt-apply"
  path="scripts/terragrunt-apply.sh"
  title="Terragrunt Apply"
  description="Apply the infrastructure changes to deploy OpenClaw on EC2."
  successMessage="OpenClaw deployed successfully!"
  failMessage="Terragrunt apply failed. See logs for details."
  inputsId={["gen-unit", "gen-module"]}
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

## Save Your SSH Key

The deployment automatically created an EC2 key pair. Save the private key to your local machine so you can SSH into the instance.

<Command
  id="save-ssh-key"
  path="scripts/save-ssh-key.sh"
  title="Save SSH Key"
  description="Extract the auto-generated SSH private key from Terragrunt state and save it locally."
  successMessage="SSH key saved!"
  failMessage="Failed to save SSH key. Check the logs for details."
  inputsId={["gen-unit", "gen-module"]}
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

You can SSH into the instance with:

<TemplateInline id="ssh-snippet">
```bash
{{ .outputs.save_ssh_key.ssh_command }}
```
</TemplateInline>

## Set up Tailscale on Your Machine

Your OpenClaw instance is now running and connected to your Tailscale network. Follow these steps to access it from your local machine.

### 1. Install the Tailscale Client

Install Tailscale on your local machine:

- **macOS**: `brew install tailscale` or download from [tailscale.com/download](https://tailscale.com/download)
- **Linux**: `curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh`
- **Windows**: Download from [tailscale.com/download](https://tailscale.com/download)

### 2. Authenticate to Your Tailnet

Run the following command and follow the browser prompt to log in:

```bash
sudo tailscale up
```

### 3. Verify the EC2 Node is Connected

Check that your OpenClaw EC2 instance appears in your Tailscale network:

```bash
tailscale status
```

You should see a node with the instance name you configured (e.g., `openclaw`). You can also verify in the [Tailscale Admin Console](https://login.tailscale.com/admin/machines).

### 4. Access OpenClaw

Open your browser and navigate to:

<TemplateInline id="access-snippet" inputsId="gen-unit">
```
http://{{ .outputs.save_ssh_key.tailscale_ip }}:{{ .inputs.GatewayPort }}
```
</TemplateInline>

### 5. Retrieve the Gateway Auth Token

The gateway auth token was auto-generated during deployment. Retrieve it via SSH:

<TemplateInline id="token-snippet">
```bash
ssh -i {{ .outputs.save_ssh_key.key_path }} ubuntu@{{ .outputs.save_ssh_key.tailscale_ip }} cat /home/ubuntu/.openclaw-token
```
</TemplateInline>

Use this token to authenticate with the OpenClaw gateway.

## Verify the Deployment

<Check
  id="test-openclaw"
  path="checks/test-openclaw.sh"
  title="Verify OpenClaw Deployment"
  description="Check that the EC2 instance is running and OpenClaw is operational."
  successMessage="OpenClaw is deployed and running!"
  failMessage="Verification failed. Check the logs for details."
  inputsId="gen-unit"
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>

## Cleanup (Optional)

When you're done with OpenClaw, you can tear down all the infrastructure created by this runbook. This will delete the EC2 instance, VPC, Elastic IP, security group, and key pair.

<Command
  id="terragrunt-destroy"
  path="scripts/terragrunt-destroy.sh"
  title="Terragrunt Destroy"
  description="Destroy all OpenClaw infrastructure. This action is irreversible."
  successMessage="All OpenClaw resources have been destroyed."
  failMessage="Terragrunt destroy failed. See logs for details."
  inputsId={["gen-unit", "gen-module"]}
  awsAuthId="aws-auth"
/>
